title: Panorra API Go New Project Report

issues.display_title: Issue #356: Elastic Connection Singleton + Fatal Removal
issues.main_description_html:
RESULT 19/06/26

✅ Implementation Complete — Ready for Unit Testing :
8ae0153

Scope: Elasticsearch connection handling only (option C from connection audit). Date: 2026-06-19

Problem

  1. Client created per request. ConnectElastic() calls elasticsearch.NewClient(cfg) on every invocation — 34 call sites + 5 duplicate ConnectElastic definitions. Each new client builds its own HTTP transport/pool, throwing away keep-alive and forcing a fresh TCP+TLS handshake per request. The *elasticsearch.Client is goroutine-safe and meant to be created once and shared.
  2. log.Fatal / log.Fatalf in the request path (16 occurrences). log.Fatal* calls os.Exit(1), so a single ES decode/get/marshal error kills the entire API process — a reliability bug.

Out of scope: RabbitMQ channel leak, Redis KEYS, postgres prefork pool sizing.

Goals

  • One shared *elasticsearch.Client per process, reused across all requests.
  • Zero log.Fatal* in the ES request path — convert to non-fatal log + error return.
  • Keep ConnectElastic() signature so the 34 call sites stay untouched.
  • Add a request timeout so a slow ES can't hang a fiber worker indefinitely.

Design

  1. Singleton in src/Utils/ElasticSearch/ElasticSearch.go — package-level cached client guarded by sync.Once; add InitElastic() (eager warmup) and keep ConnectElastic() (lazy init, signature unchanged).
  2. Remove log.Fatal* from request path (16 sites):
    • ElasticSearch.go — 6× in RetrieveElastic/RetrieveElasticV1 → log.Printf + return nil, err.
    • ElasticPost.go (4×), ElasticUsers.go (4×), ElasticComment.go (2×) — ToMap() errors → log.Println + skip merge, continue. A ToMap() failure must not abort the index write.
  3. Eager init at startup in src/Client/Client.go RunServer, next to Redis.InitRedis() — non-fatal.
  4. Request timeout — replace context.Background() with context.WithTimeout(..., 5*time.Second) in RetrieveElastic/RetrieveElasticV1 (optionally SetDocument).
  5. Workers (PostComplete, MentionPost, UpdatePostVisibility) — wrap each ConnectElastic() in sync.Once. Worker startup-path log.Fatal calls (dial/channel/queue) left unchanged (acceptable).

Testing / Verification

  • go build ./... passes.
  • grep -rn "log.Fatal" src/Utils/ElasticSearch → 0 results.
  • grep -rc "elasticsearch.NewClient" src/Utils/ElasticSearch/ElasticSearch.go → 1 (the singleton).
  • Manual: hit an ES endpoint repeatedly; confirm one client is reused and a forced ES error logs + returns instead of crashing.

Rollout Risk

Low. Signature preserved → no call-site churn. Main behavioral change: errors that previously crashed now log + return — strictly safer.

Source: docs/task/2026-06-19-elastic-connection-singleton-design.md

issues.display_title: Issue #357: RabbitMQ Connection Hardening & Publish Refactor
issues.main_description_html:
RESULT 19/06/26

✅ Implementation Complete

RabbitMQ Connection Hardening & Publish Refactor

Summary

Centralized RabbitMQ message publishing into one helper and made the shared connection reconnect-aware, removing log.Fatal crashes from the request path.

Changes

src/Utils/RabbitMQ/RabbitMQ.go

  • Added Publish(queueName, body string) error — gets a live connection, opens a channel, defer-closes it, publishes one message, and guards a nil connection (returns an error instead of panicking).
  • Rewrote GetConnection to be reconnect-aware: replaced sync.Once with a sync.Mutex; re-dials when the connection is nil or IsClosed(); returns nil on dial failure so callers handle it gracefully.

HTTP handlers switched to RabbitMQ.Publish(...) (collapsed the ~10-line channel/defer/publish boilerplate into a single call, preserving each file's error-response style):

  • Users: OtpDelete, ContactUs, ForgotPassword, ResendOtp
  • Auth/Login, Register/RegisterUser, Report/ReportBug
  • Supports: BannedPost, BannedComment, BannedUser (2 sites)
  • Notifications/PushNotification
  • Posts: DownloadUploadFileGDV1, EditPostDescription, UpdatePostVideo
  • Controllers/V1/Posts/PostsHandlers (2 sites)

Bonus fixes

  • DownloadUploadFileGDV1: replaced log.Fatal on JSON marshal with an error response; removed the now-unused log import.
  • OtpDelete / ReportBug: removed a latent nil-channel defer panic path.

Problems fixed

  1. log.Fatal in handlers (os.Exit(1)) crashed the whole API on a single failed publish.
  2. sync.Once singleton never recovered after a failed first dial or a dropped connection.
  3. ~14 handlers duplicated the same publish boilerplate with inconsistent error handling.
  4. Nil-channel defer Disconnect panic in OtpDelete / ReportBug.

Intentionally skipped

NotificationVillume, NotificationTourmkr, NotificationYoutube open one channel and publish in a loop (channel reuse); the per-call helper would open/close a channel per iteration, so they were left as-is.

Out of scope (separate task)

Remaining log.Fatal cleanup: MinIO client init across handlers, Functions.go crypto helpers, EmailTemplate.go, all Worker/* connection setup, and RabbitMQ.ConsumeQueue.

Verification

  • go build ./... — passes
  • go vet ./src/... — clean
  • gofmt — all edited files formatted

Task: RabbitMQ Connection Hardening & Publish Refactor

Branch: fix/elastic-singleton

Goal

Stop a single bad request or a dropped broker connection from crashing the API process or permanently breaking message publishing. Remove the repeated "open channel / defer close / publish" boilerplate scattered across HTTP handlers by centralizing it in one helper.

Background / Problems Found

  1. log.Fatal in request path — handlers called log.Fatal on RabbitMQ channel/publish errors. log.Fatal calls os.Exit(1), so one failed publish killed the entire API server for all users.
  2. Singleton never reconnects — GetConnection used sync.Once. If the first amqp.Dial failed, Connection stayed nil forever (Once never re-runs). If the connection later dropped (network blip / broker restart), Connection was non-nil but closed, so every conn.Channel() returned "channel/connection is not open" with no recovery until process restart.
  3. Duplicated boilerplate — the same ~10-line block (get channel, guard error, defer Disconnect, publish, guard error) was copy-pasted across ~14 handlers, with inconsistent error handling.
  4. Latent nil panic — OtpDelete and ReportBug logged the channel error but did not return, then ran defer Disconnect(channel) on a possibly nil channel → potential panic.

Changes

src/Utils/RabbitMQ/RabbitMQ.go

  • Added Publish(queueName, body string) error — gets a live connection, opens a channel, defer-closes it, publishes one message. Guards nil connection (returns an error instead of panicking).
  • Rewrote GetConnection to be reconnect-aware: replaced sync.Once with a sync.Mutex; re-dials when Connection is nil or IsClosed(); returns nil on dial failure (callers/Publish handle it gracefully).
  • GetConnectionToronto left unchanged (unused).

HTTP handlers — switched to RabbitMQ.Publish(...)

Collapsed the channel/defer/publish boilerplate into a single Publish call, preserving each file's existing error-response style:

  • src/Action/V1/Users/OtpDelete.go
  • src/Action/V1/Users/ContactUs.go
  • src/Action/V1/Users/ForgotPassword.go
  • src/Action/V1/Users/ResendOtp.go
  • src/Action/V1/Auth/Login.go
  • src/Action/V1/Register/RegisterUser.go
  • src/Action/V1/Report/ReportBug.go
  • src/Action/V1/Supports/BannedPost.go
  • src/Action/V1/Supports/BannedComment.go
  • src/Action/V1/Supports/BannedUser.go (2 sites)
  • src/Action/V1/Notifications/PushNotification.go
  • src/Action/V1/Posts/DownloadUploadFileGDV1.go
  • src/Action/V1/Posts/EditPostDescription.go
  • src/Action/V1/Posts/UpdatePostVideo.go
  • src/Controllers/V1/Posts/PostsHandlers.go (2 sites)

Bonus fixes

  • DownloadUploadFileGDV1.go: replaced log.Fatal on JSON marshal error with an error response; removed the now-unused log import.
  • OtpDelete.go / ReportBug.go: removed the nil-channel defer panic path.

Intentionally Skipped

  • NotificationVillume.go, NotificationTourmkr.go, NotificationYoutube.go — these open one channel and publish inside a loop (channel reuse). Using the per-call Publish helper would open/close a channel per iteration (wasteful), so they were left as-is.

Out of Scope (remaining log.Fatal cleanup — separate task)

Still crash the process and should be migrated to error responses later:

  • MinIO client init log.Fatalln across many handlers (AddPost, DeletePost, EditProfile, UploadPanorama*, RegisterGoogle, CollectionPost, etc.)
  • src/Utils/Functions/Functions.go (crypto helpers)
  • src/Utils/EmailTemplate/EmailTemplate.go (8 sites)
  • All src/Worker/* connection/channel/queue log.Fatal
  • src/Utils/RabbitMQ/RabbitMQ.go ConsumeQueue log.Fatal

Verification

  • go build ./... — passes
  • go vet ./src/... — clean
  • gofmt — all edited files formatted
  • Runtime smoke test: publish a message; kill broker; confirm next request reconnects instead of erroring permanently.

Suggested Commits

  1. feat: add RabbitMQ.Publish helper + reconnect-aware GetConnection
  2. refactor: use RabbitMQ.Publish across HTTP handlers
